_doc: Performance budgets for the perf-guardrails skill. Consumed by
(priority checklist). Two levels per metric, per spec D2 (.agents/specs/playwright-e2e.md):
budget  — advisory. A breach is reported in .agents/reports/perf/last-run.json and
raises the route's priority in the triage checklist. It never fails a test.
ceiling — blocking. Generous enough that only a real regression (an N+1 explosion,
a hung query, a render stall) trips it; a breach fails the perf test.
Units: *_ms are milliseconds; queries is DB statements per request (deterministic, so
its ceiling can be tight). backend_ms is measured SERVER-SIDE (request_started ->
request_finished inside the app process), not client wall-clock. lcp_ms is Chromium's
largest-contentful-paint on a warm cache, median of 3 — a local regression tripwire,
NOT a real-user metric; production LCP truth is the Faro RUM web-vitals stream.
Calibrated 2026-07-18 from a real run on an empty-org baseline (last-run.json):
API medians 3.6-34ms, page shells ~4ms, LCP 56-108ms, queries 2-11. Budgets sit at
roughly 4x the worst observed value; ceilings where only a real regression lands.
The /api/core/dashboard/summary override RATCHETS its known 38-query debt: any new
query trips the advisory, and the number must only ever go down. Recalibrate with the
procedure in the SKILL.md rather than hand-tuning numbers to make a red run green.
